western.definition: Replicability through standardized methods; control of variables in isolated systems; statistical significance.

relational.definition: Reproducible results using systematic methods; multi-modal verification; practical effectiveness over time.
assumptions: Rigor through verification, not through instrument mediation alone.

hidden_variables.name: Sensor reliability regardless of type
hidden_variables.description: Whether a given sensor (biological or instrumental) actually produces consistent, accurate readings in deployment.
hidden_variables.why_western_misses_it: Acceptance is gated on sensor category, not sensor performance.

hidden_variables.name: Contextual factors
hidden_variables.description: Field conditions that affect whether the result generalizes.

hidden_variables.name: Long-term outcomes
hidden_variables.description: Whether the predicted result holds across the relevant timescale.
hidden_variables.why_western_misses_it: Study durations are bounded by funding and publication cycles.

hidden_variables.name: Relationship quality
hidden_variables.description: Trust and cooperation between researcher and the system under study (including communities, ecosystems, and collaborators).
hidden_variables.why_western_misses_it: Treated as bias to be eliminated rather than infrastructure to be maintained.

translation_protocol.when_to_translate: When 'rigor' is being equated with controlled-isolation methodology and field-condition reproducibility is being dismissed.
translation_protocol.how: Ask: 'What does it take for a finding to be reproducible in deployment, not just in the lab? Whose verification counts?'
